CREATE TABLE test_list (id INTEGER, values ARRAY<INTEGER>);
SELECT id, array_has(values, 1) AS has_one FROM test_list WHERE array_has(values, 1);
SELECT id, array_has(values, 4) AS has_four FROM test_list WHERE array_has(values, 4);
SELECT id, array_has(values, NULL) AS has_null FROM test_list WHERE array_has(values, NULL);
SELECT id FROM test_list WHERE array_has(values, 10);
SELECT id, array_has(values, 6) AS has_six FROM test_list WHERE array_has(values, 6) OR id = 2;
SELECT t.id, array_has(t.values, 2) FROM test_list AS t JOIN test_list AS t2 ON t.id = t2.id WHERE array_has(t2.values, 4);
SELECT id FROM test_list WHERE array_has(values, (SELECT MAX(value) FROM (SELECT UNNEST(values) AS value WHERE id = 1)));
SELECT DISTINCT id FROM test_list WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM (SELECT UNNEST(values) AS elem) tmp WHERE elem = 3 AND tmp.id = test_list.id) AND array_has(values, 2);
SELECT id, array_has(ARRAY[5, 6, 7], 6) AS contains_six, array_has(ARRAY[5, 6, 7], 8) AS contains_eight FROM test_list;
